original: Saint Zosimus begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Edward the Martyr, the teenage King of England, is murdered, possibly arranged by his stepmother Queen Ælfthryth, by Corfe Castle
First Council of the Lateran (9th ecumenical council) opens in Rome, agreements of the Concordat of Worms are ratified
Battle of al-Babein: King Amalric I of Jerusalem fights a Zengid army to a draw over access to Egypt
Battle of Ichi-no-Tani near Kobe, Japan
Crusaders kill 57 Jews in Bury St Edmunds, England
Holy Roman Emperor Frederick II crowns himself King of Jerusalem
Mongolian armies rout the forces of Boleslaw IV at Chmielnik and sack and burn the city of Kraków to the ground
According to legend, Tenochtitlan is founded on this date on an island in what was then Lake Texcoco in the Valley of Mexico
Albert II of Habsburg chosen as King of Germany at Frankfurt
Parliament of England bans payments by the English Church to Rome
Dutch States General and Anjou sign treaty
Susenyos is formally crowned Emperor of Ethiopia
Lord Berkley sells his half of New Jersey to the Quakers
Britain repeals the Stamp Act, which had caused outrage in colonial America and helped lead to the American Revolution
Oliver Goldsmith's "She Stoops to Conquer" premieres in London
Second Battle at Neerwinden: Austria army beats France
The first republican state in Germany, the Republic of Mainz, is declared by Andreas Joseph Hofmann
David Melville, Newport, Rhode Island, patents apparatus for making coal gas
US Congress approves 1st pensions for government service
1st railroad tunnel in US completed, in Pennsylvania (275 m long)
Six farm laborers from Tolpuddle in Dorset, England, are sentenced to transportation to Australia for forming a trade union
In a bloody battle Liberal general Francisco Morazán attempts to retake Guatemala City from Rafael Carrera but fails, barely escaping with his life
First Dutch public telegram
Henry Wells & William Fargo form American Express in Buffalo
Dutch Van der Brugghen government resigns
Vera Cruz besieged by Miramon (Cons) in Mexican War of Reform
Battle of Wilson's raid to Selma, Alabama
Congress of Confederate States of American adjourns for last time
Antonio Carlos Gomes' "Il Guarany" premieres at La Scala Teatro in Milan, Kingdom of Italy, the first Brazilian opera successful outside Brazil
Lake Merritt in Oakland, California, becomes the first US National Wildlife Refuge
Communards revolt in Paris
Hawaii signs a treaty with the United States, granting the US exclusive trading rights
City of Anaheim in California incorporates for the 2nd time
Morgan Earp is assassinated by outlaws while playing billiards in Tombstone
Britain is linked to the continent by Telephone
Lord Stanley presents silver challenge cup for hockey (Stanley Cup)
200 African Americans leave Savannah, Georgia for Liberia
Phoebe, a moon of Saturn is discovered by William Pickering
Ajax soccer club forms in Amsterdam, The Netherlands (33 Eredivisie titles, 18 KNVB Cups, 4 Champions League); named after legendary Greek hero
Japan uses its influence over Korea to deny Russia's efforts to obtain a naval station at Korean Port of Masampo, the lead up to the Russo-Japanese war
Turkey grants Germany syndicate the first concession to construct a railroad through Turkish territory to Baghdad, to be linked to Berlin
Following through on its attacks on Roman Catholic institutions, the French Government dissolves the Catholic religious orders
Einar Dessau of Denmark makes 1st ham broadcast
Russia and Bulgaria reach an agreement in which late 19th century Russian financial claims are cancelled to meet compensation due to Turkey from Bulgaria
Frederick Converse's opera "The Pipe of Desire" is the first American opera to be performed at the Metropolitan Opera, NYC
North Dakota enacts a hail insurance law
King George I of Greece is assassinated in the recently liberated city of Thessaloniki
White Wolf gang beats government army in Jingdezhen, China
Failed British attack in Dardanelles
French battleship Bouvet explodes, 640 killed
Soccer team SON OF Meerssen forms
Socialist Youth AJC organizes in Amsterdam
Order of DeMolay forms in Kansas City
2nd Peace of Riga, Poland enlarged
Steamer "Hong Koh" runs aground off Swatow China killing 1,000
1st intercollegiate indoor polo championship (Princeton vs Yale)
The first public celebration of Bat mitzvah, for the daughter of Rabbi Mordecai Kaplan, is held in New York City.
WBT-AM in Charlotte NC begins radio transmissions

Great Tri-State Tornado: Monstrous F5 (over 300MPH) tornado roars 219 miles across southeast Missouri, southern Illinois, and southwest Indiana; kills 695, injures over 2000, and destroys 15,000 homes
Boston Bruins win record 20th NHL home game
1st electric shavers go on sale in US (Schick)
Juan Bautista Aznar becomes premier of Spain
Radio Clube de Mocambique's 1st radio transmission
Gas explosion in school in New London, Texas: 294 die
The human-powered aircraft, Pedaliante, flies 1 kilometre (0.62 miles) outside Milan.
NY 1st requires serological blood tests of pregnant women
President Cardena of Mexico nationalizes US & British oil companies
Illegal Free Netherlands announces boycott of theaters
British merchant vessel "Canadian Star" is torpedoed and sunk in the Atlantic by German U-boat U-221, 34 die with 54 survivors
Red Army evacuates Belgorod
Ships James Oglethorpe (US) & Terkolei (Netherlands) torpedoed and sinks
Nazi Germany occupies Hungary
US Army Air Force completes largest bombing raid on Berlin Germany: Over 1200 bombers drop 3,000 tons of explosives
US Task Force 58 attacks targets on Kyushu, Japan
France, Great Britain, and Benelux sign Treaty of Brussels
Philips begin experimental TV broadcasting
WGAL TV channel 8 in Lancaster, PA (NBC) begins broadcasting
CCNY beats Bradley 69-61 for the NIT championship
William Grant Still's Symphony No. 4 ("Autochthonous"), premiere  performance, by the Oklahoma City Symphony Orchestra with Victor Alessandro conducting
1st plastic lens for cataract patients fitted (Philadelphia)
7.5 magnitude earthquake strikes Yenice–Gönen in western Turkey, 1070 die
KGNC (now KAMR) TV channel 4 in Amarillo, TX (NBC) begins broadcasting
MLB National League approves Boston Braves move to Milwaukee (1st shift since 1903)
Ichirō Hatojama recognized as premier of Japan
WTWV (now WTVA) TV channel 9 in Tupelo-Columbus, MS (NBC) begins
MLB Los Angeles Dodgers announce their mascot and clown Emmett Kelly will not perform in 1958
Dmitri Shostakovichbecomes member of Supreme Soviet of USSR
France performs underground nuclear test at Ecker, Algeria
WGSF TV channel 31 in Newark, OH (PBS) begins broadcasting
A truck loses control down Moosic Street, Scranton, Pennsylvania, killing the driver. This accident later inspired the 1974 Harry Chapin song, "30,000 Pounds of Bananas."
Poppin' Fresh Pillsbury Dough Boy introduced
Rolling Stones fined £5 each for public urination
USSR launches Voshkod 2
Scott Paper begins selling paper dresses for $1
Oil tanker Torrey Canyon hits a rock off Cornwall & spills oil
the Beatles' single "Penny Lane" single goes #1
US Congress repeals requirement for a gold reserve
WVER TV channel 28 in Rutland, VT (PBS) begins broadcasting
WVTA TV channel 41 in Windsor, VT (PBS) begins broadcasting
Cambodia military coup under General Lon Nol, Prince Sihanuk flees
Five Unionist Members of Parliament (MPs), including William Craig and Harry West, are expelled from the Unionist Parliamentary Party, Northern Ireland
KAEC TV channel 19 in Lufkin, Texas (ABC) suspends broadcasting
NFL selects Wilson as official football & scoreboard as official time
Two-week US postal strike begins; it is against the government and is the largest wildcat strike in US history
200 die in landslide into Lake Yanahuani, Chungar Peru
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
Cornell NCAA hockey team shut out for 1st time in 225 games (Boston U)
Memphis' Larry Miller sets ABA record of 67 pts in a game
Ulster Vanguard hold a rally of 60,000 people in Belfast; William Craig tells the crowd: "if and when the politicians fail us, it may be our job to liquidate the enemy"
Most Arab oil producing nations end embargo against US
Kurds end fight against Iraqi army
The Clash release their first recording "White Riot"
US restricts citizens from visiting Cuba, Vietnam, North Korea & Cambodia
Vietnam hands over MIA to US
250,000 attend rock concert California Jam II in Ontario, California
Former Pakistani premier Ali Bhutto sentenced to death
Battles between Kurds & Iranians break in Sananday, Iran
Vostok rocket explodes on the launch pad while being refueled, killing 50
Buffalo Sabres sets NHL record of 9 goals in 1 period (vs Toronto)
Singer Teddy Pendergrass' spinal cord severed in a car accident
Capital Cities Communications Inc. acquires ABC for $3.5 billion in the first transfer of ownership of a TV network
US Treasury Department announces plans to alter paper money
Gerber survey find most popular names for newborns are Jessica and Matthew
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
27th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Discovery 8 (STS-29), returns to Earth
California Quake amusement ride opens at Universal Studios
In Egypt, a 4,400-year-old mummy is found in the Pyramid of Cheops
1st free elections in East Germany, Conservatives beat Communists
32-day lockout by baseball owners ends
A Tampa little leaguer dies after being struck by a pitch
In the largest art heist in US history, 13 works of art worth over $500 million are stolen from the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum in Boston
Reggie Miller (Indiana Pacers) ends NBA free throw streak of 52 games
American businesswoman Leona Helmsley sentenced to 4 years for tax evasion "We don't pay taxes; only the little people pay taxes"
Zimbabwe beat England by 9 runs in World Cup at Albury
Amsterdam stock exchange hits record Ÿ12.2 billion
Sri Lanka beat England in Test match by 5 wickets
South Africa Goldstone committee reveals existence of secret police
Space shuttle STS-62 (Columbia 16), lands
STS 67 (Endeavour 8) lands after 16½ days
50,000 swimmers raise 15 million for charity during BT's Swimathon '96
A nightclub fire in Quezon City, Philippines, kills 162 people

Ben & Jerry's introduce the philanthropic flavor "Phish Food" in conjunction with American jam band Phish at the  Flynn Theatre in Burlington, Vermont; some proceeds from the chocolate ice cream with marshmallows, caramel and fish-shaped fudge. are donated to the band's charitable Water Wheel Foundation
Russian AN-24 plane crashes in Turkey, killing 50 people
British Sign Language is recognised as an official British language
FBI agents raid the corporate headquarters of HealthSouth Corporation in Birmingham, Alabama on suspicion of massive corporate fraud led by the company's top executives
Terri Schiavo's feeding tube is removed at the request of her husband

Former England Test cricket batsman, and Pakistan coach Bob Woolmer is found dead one day after his team's defeat to Ireland puts them out of the World Cup in the West Indies; cause of death remains suspicious
MESSENGER spacecraft enters Mercury's orbit
Joachim Gauck elected President of the Federal republic of Germany by the Federal Assembly
Superleague Greece football match between Olympiacos and Panathinaikos is abandoned after fans set fire to Athens' Olympic Stadium
98 people are killed and 248 are injured across Iraq from a series of bombings and shootings
A car bombing kills 10 people and injures 20 in Mogadishu, Somalia
Explosions kill 25 people at a bus park in Kano, Nigeria
Russia formally annexes Crimea, previously part of Ukraine. by signing Treaty on Accession
Transnistria formally requests to join the Russian Federation
US closes the Syrian embassy in Washington & expels all Syrian diplomats
African American Stephon Clark shot 20 times by police in his Grandmother's backyard in Sacramento, California during vandalism investigation
Bushfire in Tathra, New South Wales, Australia, destroys over 70 buildings
First fatal accident involving an Uber self-driving car hitting a pedestrian in Tempe, Arizona
Serial bomber suspected after fourth bomb goes off in Austin, Texas, injuring two, total bombing death toll, 2 dead, 5 injured
Turkish-backed Syrian rebels seize Kurdish held northern Syrian town of Afrin
40-year old Dallas Mavericks forward Dirk Nowitzki overtakes Wilt Chamberlain to go 6th with 31,424 points on the NBA scorers' list; Mavs suffer 129-125 OT loss to New Orleans Pelicans
Champion racing pigeon Armando "the Lewis Hamilton of pigeons" sells for a record $1.42 million in Belgium
Italian charity ship Mare Jonio rescues 50 migrants off the coast of Libya
Mozambique President Filipe Nyusi makes national radio address to the nation after Cyclone Idai, saying more than 1,000 people feared dead with 100,000 at risk
Suspected terrorist attack by shooter aboard a tram in Utrecht, Netherlands kills 3 and injures 5
“Historic to catastrophic flooding" faced by Nebraska, Iowa, Minnesota, Wisconsin and South Dakota according to US National Weather Service
Turing Award given to Pixar employees Pat Hanraha and Edwin Catmull for three-dimensional computer graphics
US House Judiciary Committee hearing begins on rise of violence and discrimination against Asian-Americans with report saying 3,800 hate incidents recorded over 12 months
US Senate confirms William Joseph Burns as new CIA Director
Israel launches airstrike attacks on Gaza, killing at least 404 people and injuring 562 others, ending the ceasefire agreement reached in January
NASA astronauts Suni Williams and Butch Wilmore finally return to Earth from the International Space Station after nine months, instead of one week, aboard a SpaceX capsule
Roman Senate annulsTiberius' will and proclaims Gaius Julius Caesar Augustus Germanicus (akaCaligula= Little Boots) emperor
Jacques de Molay, the 23rd and last Grand Master of the Knights Templar, is burned at the stake by KingPhilip IV of France
Emperor Maximilian Inames his daughter Margaret of Austria the first woman Governor of the Netherlands and regent to her brotherCharles V
PrinceWilliam I of Orangeinjured in attack at Antwerp
Renown French preacherJacques-Bénigne Bossuetordained as a priest, having been named Archdeacon of Sarrebourg
Thomas Pelham-Hollesbecomes British Prime Minister on the death of his brotherHenry Pelham
French astronomerCharles Messierrediscovers global cluster M92
Robert Burnspoem “Tam o’ Shanter,” is published in the "Edinburgh Herald"
US PresidentRutherford B. HayesappointsFrederick Douglassmarshal of Washington, D.C.
Barnum & BaileyCircus, traveling as "The Greatest Show on Earth", debuts at Madison Square Garden in New York City following the merger of two existing circus groups
German ChancellorOtto von Bismarckresigns after 19 years after a disagreement with German EmperorWilhelm II
Arnold Schoenberg's "Verklärte Nacht" premieres in Vienna
Italian operatic tenorEnrico Carusobecomes the first well-known performer to make a record
First performance ofEdward Elgar's "In the South (Alassio)"
British magistrates in India sentenceMahatma Gandhito six years imprisonment for disobedience
Silent film classic "The Thief of Bagdad" based on One Thousand and One Nights, starringDouglas Fairbanksand directed by Raoul Walsh is released
Russian premiere of silent film "The New Babylon" premieres in Leningrad, written and directed by Grigori Kozintsev and Leonid Trauberg with a score byDmitri Shostakovich
Benito MussoliniandAdolf Hitlermeet at Brenner Pass, where the Italian dictator agrees to join Germany's impending war effort in the west
Two Black players,Jackie Robinsonand Nate Moreland, request a tryout with the Chicago White Sox and are allowed to work out
US PresidentFranklin D. Rooseveltsigns Executive Order 9102, creating the War Relocation Authority, which was charged with overseeing the internment of Japanese Americans during World War II
Montreal CanadienMaurice "Rocket" Richardbecomes the 1st NHLer to score 50 goals
Soviet consultants leave Yugoslavia in first sign ofTito-Stalinsplit
Boston Celtic'sBill Sharmanbegins record of 56 straight free-throws
US PresidentDwight D. Eisenhowersigns Hawaii statehood bill
Algerian War of Independence ends in a ceasefire after 7½ yrs and 250,000 dead,Ahmed Ben Bellaflees
General Suhartoforms government in Indonesia
CommissionerPeter UeberrothreinstatesWillie Mays&Mickey Mantle
Dino Ciccarellisets Washington Capital record of 7 pts in a game
Mike Tysonbeats Razor Ruddock in the 7th round
Philadelphia '76ers retiresWilt Chamberlain's #13 jersey
American pop singerDonna Summergets a star on Hollywood's Walk of Fame
Zsa Zsa Gaborfiles for bankruptcy
Michael Jordanannounces he is ending his 17 month NBA retirement

Apple Records releases The Beatles "Anthology 2" (double CD/triple LP)", the second of 3-part series of rare recordings and outtakes by the Beatles; set covers the years 1965-68, as well as new collaboration "Real Love" - aJohn Lennondemo augmented by additional lyrics and performances by the surviving three; album tops record charts in US and UK
17th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees:Isaac Hayes; Brenda Lee;Tom Pettyand the Heartbreakers; Gene Pitney;Ramones; Talking Heads; Chet Atkins; and Jim Stewart
"The Suite Life of Zack & Cody" premieres on the Disney Channel, starring Dylan andCole Sprouse
Phil Jacksonsigns a five-year contract to be president of the New York Knicks
Portuguese soccer superstarCristiano Ronaldorecords his 50th career hat-trick in Real Madrid's 6-3 win over Girona
US Senator Amy Klobuchar calls onMark Zuckerbergto testify before the Senate Judiciary in wake in data mining scandal with Cambridge Analytica
Vladimir Putinis elected to a new six-year term as Russian President with 76% of the vote, his fourth term
UK Speaker of the House John Bercow rules a third vote on Prime MinisterTheresa May's Brexit deal is not possible without new changes
US PresidentDonald Trumpand Canadian Prime MinisterJustin Trudeauagree to close the US-Canada border, the world's longest, to non-essential travel to curb COVID-19
US PresidentJoe Bidenagrees Russian PresidentVladimir Putinis a "killer" in ABC News interview. Putin responds "It takes one to know one" a day later.
First public bus service begins, promoted byBlaise Pascal, operates in Paris as the "Carosses à Cinq Sous" until 1675
Greece adopts the Gregorian calendar
Soviet cosmonautAlexei Leonovleaves his spacecraft Voskhod 2 for 12 minutes and becomes the first person to conduct a spacewalk
